questions.questionText: What is the first step before beginning a WISP installation at a client's location?
options: Start crimping the LAN cable
Climb the roof and install the antenna
Perform a site survey and line-of-sight check
Install the router inside the house

questions.questionText: Why is it important to secure the antenna firmly during installation?
options: To prevent it from being stolen
To maintain stable alignment and avoid signal loss due to wind or movement
To ensure it powers the router properly
So that rainwater does not affect signal strength

questions.questionText: What should you always do before crimping a LAN cable for a client installation?
options: Measure the client's internet speed
Strip the cable without untwisting the wire pairs too much
Insert the cable directly into the router
Test the cable with a multimeter

questions.questionText: Which direction should excess cable be routed during cable laying?
options: Across walkways for easy access
Dangling freely from the rooftop
Tucked neatly along corners or conduits
Through windows or door hinges

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of using cable clips or conduits during installation?
options: To make installation faster
To improve internet speed
To reduce cable costs
To protect and neatly secure the cable throughout the route

questions.questionText: Why is it important to test the router and connection inside the house after setup?
options: To update its firmware
To register the user on the ISP's billing system
To ensure the client is receiving stable internet before leaving
To pair it with the antenna wirelessly

questions.questionText: What is a key step in the Signal Optimization process?
options: Upgrading the router firmware
Crimping another backup cable
Pointing the antenna toward the nearest access point while monitoring signal strength
Switching frequencies every 30 seconds

questions.questionText: What kind of maintenance task is typically performed after installation?
options: Repainting the antenna
Changing the client's password monthly
Checking signal quality and inspecting for physical damage or cable wear
Reinstalling the router every month

questions.questionText: Why should installers avoid sharp bends in LAN cables during installation?
options: It makes the installation look messy
Sharp bends can degrade signal quality or damage internal wires
It makes crimping more difficult
Clients might complain about the color of the cable

questions.questionText: What should be done if a crimped cable fails a continuity test?
options: Still use it if it fits into the port
Trim and recrimp both ends properly and test again
Try another frequency band
Restart the router repeatedly
